journal.setup_type: Standard Supply/Demand Retest (taken below the R:R floor)
journal.result: LOSS - SL tagged cleanly on the 07-16 13:00 UTC candle (H 0.0017823, well above SL 0.001727), exactly -1.0R. Entry 0.00165, SL 0.001727, realized loss = (0.001727-0.00165)/(0.001727-0.00165) = -1R exactly, a full clean stop-out, no slippage beyond the level.
journal.note: This trade was correctly identified as sub-floor (1.63R at the honest TP1 vs. the 2R Intraday floor) and taken anyway with eyes open - the loss does not invalidate the entry mechanics (which were sound: real confirmed rejection, limit filled on a retest, not a chase), it validates the R:R floor discipline itself. This is the clean counterfactual to JTO's win: same class of process gap (trade taken below/against the rules), opposite outcome - proof the floor exists for a reason, not proof the floor was wrong. Price broke out hard after the stop, clearing a 16-day high and continuing toward a much larger 0.0180-0.0188 resistance zone (dozens of touches, April-June) - reanalyzed fresh 07-16 17:10 UTC as WAIT/NO CHASE, no new entry until price reaches and reacts to that zone.

journal.setup_type: Breaker / Failed Supply-Demand Flip / Compression-to-Expansion Breakout

journal.result: Invalidated thesis - the Jul 9 conditional long lost the 0.0185-0.0190 demand zone and tagged the old 0.0178 SL on Jul 15. No user entry was reported.

journal.result: WIN - closed manually at 0.6051, entry 0.665, SL 0.695 (never touched). Realized ~2.0R, which cleared the corrected TP1 (0.6076) despite the exit being a manual close rather than a TP fill - 0.6051 is a better price than 0.6076 for a short. Held roughly three days (entered 07-13, closed 07-16) through two separate zone tests (a wick-only reclaim sweep above 0.68 that got rejected, and a later sweep-and-reclaim of the TP1 zone itself) without the thesis ever actually invalidating (SL untouched throughout).
journal.note: This trade carries two separate, opposite lessons that should not be merged into one takeaway. (1) Entry timing was still premature at the start - the original limit filled ~15-30 min before the actual confirmation candle closed (documented in SCORECARD.json's JTO record), which was flagged as a real process issue at the time, independent of outcome. (2) TP levels were miscalibrated THREE times over the trade's life as the TP Zone Validity Rule and its near-edge refinement were discovered and sharpened (0.59 -> 0.5968 -> 0.6076 for TP1 alone) - every correction moved the stated R:R down, never up, which became the general pattern named explicitly in PUMPFUN's record. Despite both of these process gaps, the underlying directional thesis (short from the 0.665-0.68 reclaim-failure zone) was correct and the trade won on a real, if early, discretionary exit. Do not let a winning outcome retroactively excuse either the early entry or the TP miscalibration - grade process and outcome separately, same principle established in the scorecard for this exact trade.

journal.result: Chart's 'AutoTrader Trade Map' script source was corrupted to a garbage string, with two stacked broken instances on the chart. Not a trading mistake; APE's setup thesis was unaffected and rendered correctly once fixed.
journal.note: Recovery: reload the chart tab, reopen Pine Editor, select-all + paste correct script via a real paste event (clipboard write + native paste, or a synthetic ClipboardEvent dispatched on the Monaco .inputarea if OS clipboard permission is unavailable), save, then explicitly click Add to chart (saving alone does not reattach a removed/broken instance). Verify by reading the rendered card, not by absence of the compile-error icon alone.
